Leandra is a genus of flowering plants in the family Melastomataceae. The Leandra genus is known for its ornamental and medicinal properties. Leandra species, including Leandra cordifolia and Leandra rhodophylla, have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, such as fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, due to their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Leandra species include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other secondary metabolites.
